name: Plasencia Upstream River Trail
name_original: Paseo del pantano – circular por Cordel del Valles
description: This moderate 12.1-mile mountain biking route circles the Jerte Reservoir, offering scenic views and a good workout in 1 hour and 22 minutes
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia offer diverse terrain within the picturesque Jerte Valley, characterized by the reservoir itself and the Jerte River. The region features extensive forests of chestnut, oak, and holm oak, providing shaded routes and scenic backdrops. Mountain ranges like the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Tormantos contribute to varied topography, including gorges and waterways. This landscape provides a comprehensive network of trails for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia The most popular mountain bike trail is Plasencia Upstream River Trail, a 12.1 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the river upstream, offering a consistent ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Jerte Reservoir – Embalse de Plasencia loop from La Data, an easy 14.9 miles (24.0 km) path. This route provides expansive views of the reservoir and surrounding natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Isla Park – Embalse de Plasencia loop from Ibarruri - La Esperanza, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de Plasencia is defined by reservoir-side paths, dense forests, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Embalse de Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embalse de Plasencia?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia, offering a wide range of experiences. You'll find everything from easy, family-friendly rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains and technical sections, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect on mountain bike trails near Embalse de Plasencia?
A: The trails around Embalse de Plasencia cater to all skill levels. You can find 6 easy routes perfect for beginners or casual rides, 37 moderate trails that offer a good balance of challenge and enjoyment, and 9 difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers seeking more technical terrain and climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes suitable for families. These trails often feature mostly paved surfaces or well-maintained paths, providing a pleasant experience for riders of all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte Reservoir – Embalse de Plasencia loop from La Data&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that provides expansive views of the reservoir.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Embalse de Plasencia?
A: The Jerte Valley, where Embalse de Plasencia is located, is particularly stunning in spring (late March to early April) when the cherry trees are in blossom, creating a picturesque landscape. Autumn also offers beautiful scenery with changing foliage. Generally, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for riding, while summer can be hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints around Embalse de Plasencia?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around the reservoir provide expansive views of the water and the surrounding mountainous landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte Reservoir – Embalse de Plasencia loop from La Data&lt;/a&gt; is known for its scenic reservoir views. The wider Jerte Valley also features routes that climb into the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Tormantos, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte Reservoir – Embalse de Plasencia loop from La Data&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Park – Embalse de Plasencia loop from Ibarruri - La Esperanza&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on the mountain bike trails?
A: You'll experience a rich variety of natural features. The trails often wind through extensive forests of chestnut, oak, cork oak, and holm oak. You'll also find routes along the Jerte River, with some exploring gorges and waterways. The region is flanked by the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Tormantos, providing diverse topography.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the nearby city of Plasencia offers a rich historical and cultural complement to your ride. You can explore its well-preserved medieval center, which includes two cathedrals (13th and 15th century), ancient city walls, and the 16th-century San Lázaro Bridge over the Jerte River. These sites are easily accessible from many of the trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Embalse de Plasencia?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from reservoir-side paths to dense forests, and the comprehensive network of trails that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any specific trails for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: For advanced riders, there are 9 difficult routes available that feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009663715/camino-viejo-del-puerto-pantano-plasencia-circular-desde-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Viejo del Puerto-Pantano Plasencia – circular desde Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is one such challenging option, offering a demanding ride with considerable climbing.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking around Embalse de Plasencia?
A: While specific parking spots vary by trailhead, many routes start from or pass through areas with accessible parking, particularly near the city of Plasencia or smaller towns surrounding the reservoir. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for detailed parking information.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Jerte River?
A: Yes, the Jerte River is a significant natural feature of the region, and several mountain bike trails follow its course. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021563508/plasencia-upstream-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Upstream River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that follows the river, offering a consistent and scenic ride.
tips: The Paseo del Pantano is a beautiful trail to walk or bike. Most of the way is paved and there are benches to rest and enjoy the beauty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the Paseo del pantano – circular por Cordel del Valles?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 22 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 19.4 km circular path.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied, typical of the Valles region. You can expect a mix of surfaces, potentially including well-maintained gravel tracks and some singletrack sections. Given its name, it likely features paths around a reservoir and through more traditional mountain tracks. Some sections might be rocky or muddy, especially after rain, and there could be areas with significant tree cover as well as open sections offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Is the Paseo del pantano – circular por Cordel del Valles suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. While it's not overly long at 19.4 km with about 200 meters of elevation gain, the varied terrain and potential for some technical sections mean it's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience and a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners might find certain parts challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes scenic sections like the Paseo del Pantano and offers views of the Jerte Reservoir. Riding around reservoirs typically provides picturesque views of the water body, often surrounded by Mediterranean landscapes and forests.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While a specific official trailhead for this exact named route isn't widely documented, routes in areas like the Valles region often have parking available near popular access points or villages. It's advisable to check local maps or community forums for the most convenient parking options close to where you plan to begin your circular ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Valles region generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter can be pleasant on dry days, but be aware that some sections might become muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Arriba, and various segments of the Cordel del Valle. It also includes sections of the Enlace paseo pantano N110.

faqs.question: Is the Paseo del pantano – circular por Cordel del Valles dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas of Catalonia are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet. Check for any specific local regulations or signage at the trailhead.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: For informal or locally recognized routes like this one, permits or entrance fees are typically not required. However, if any part of the route passes through a protected natural park or private land, there might be specific regulations. It's always a good idea to check local signage or official park websites if you suspect you'll be entering a protected area.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Riding through the Mediterranean landscapes and forests around the reservoir, you might encounter common local wildlife such as various bird species, small mammals like rabbits or squirrels, and possibly reptiles. Keep an eye out for local flora, especially during spring when wildflowers are in bloom. Always observe wildlife from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources nearby?
faqs.answer: As this route is likely localized, specific amenities directly on the trail might be limited. However, the Valles region has towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and places to refill water. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride, especially since the route is moderate in difficulty and takes over an hour.
